titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Sangermanensis primus
VL7
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lk Jo

surrogates.bibl: Black and white digital images on CD in ITSEE supplied by BNF. Missing two pages in John: supplied from microfilm in INTF.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John in 2008-9.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 94r and 125r-134v. The manuscript includes a list of capitula (Type I, fol. 94r), and sortes in the margin of John.The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. Rubrication cannot be securely identified from the images. The symbols connecting the sortes (not transcribed) with the text have been transcribed as %. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

body.note: The column begins with the closing capitula of Luke.

ab.note: The first part of column one contains the end of Luke.
There is a division sign (÷) over this word, relating to a 'sors' in the margin.
There is a division sign (÷) over this word, relating to a 'sors' in the margin.
There is a division sign (÷) over this word, but no corresponding 'sors' in the margin.

ab.w:
resurrexis
Not present in BN images; transcribed from image of Muenster microfilm
set

ab.note: There may be expunction dots over 'di'.
The chapter number is in the left margin.
double dot above p of templo
This space is a possible erasure.
The rest of the line is blank, which may be a deletion.
This space is a possible erasure.
The last letter of 'uidit' appears to to be corrected.
The duplication of text has not been noticed or corrected.
There may be a hole in the parchment.
The weight of the pen is different in 'laui' and this may be a correction.
This space appears to be an erasure.
A blank line has been left here in the manuscript.
The chapter number is in the left margin.
The number '·U·' is found in the middle of the bottom margin; presumably a quire signature.
The chapter number xxui has also been added in the left margin like the Ammonian sections.
The rest of this line is written in a larger script, but not quite capitals, and may be over an erasure.
The spaces over the next 10 lines seem to be due to an imperfection in the parchment.
As on the recto, the gaps appear to be due to a deficiency in the parchment.
The correction is in the centre margin, preceded by a signe de renvoi (also indicated above 'ianuae'); 'foris' seems to be preceded by an 'a'.
The next column continues with an incipit for Acts, the Catholic Epistles and Revelation, and the capitula for Acts.
